summary.intent: Improve the moltis Microsoft Teams channel implementation by adding missing features compared to openclaw, then improve onboarding and documentation.
summary.outcome: Comprehensive Teams channel overhaul delivered: JWT auth, retry/backoff, async webhook handling, Adaptive Cards, streaming, file attachments, Graph API tools (search, pins, reactions, member info), full documentation, improved web UI with Tailscale-aware public URL detection, and a merged PR (#529).

repo: msteams crate lives at crates/msteams/src/ with modules: lib, config, activity, state, outbound, plugin, auth, channel_webhook_verifier
Agent tools are registered in crates/gateway/src/server.rs after SendMessageTool and live in crates/gateway/src/teams_agent_tools.rs
Channel descriptors (supports_threads, supports_interactive, supports_reactions) are set in crates/channels/src/plugin.rs
Default offered channels list is in crates/config/src/schema.rs with a matching test that must be updated when channels are added
RedactedConfig serialization uses a hardcoded field count that must match the actual number of always-present fields — getting this wrong causes runtime panics
Tailscale status is fetched at onboarding startup and available as data.tailscale; API endpoints are /api/tailscale/status and /api/tailscale/configure
Config validation in validate.rs uses Map(Box::new(Leaf)) for channel configs — actual field validation happens via serde deserialization, not validate.rs
Locale files are Telegram-only; all other channels have a gap in fr/zh localization
CLI bootstrap prints setup links and can open them; Teams Developer Portal link was missing and needed adding
secrecy::Secret<String> is used for credentials; outbound uses constant-time comparison for webhook secrets

code.finding: RedactedConfig had hardcoded count of 9 but there were 14 always-present fields — this would cause runtime serialization panics
std::sync::RwLockWriteGuard held across .await points causes 'not Send' compile errors; must scope guards so they drop before any await
Teams webhook must return 202 Accepted immediately and process in background via tokio::spawn to prevent Teams retry storms on slow LLM responses
splits_at_space test had wrong expected value — rfind(' ') on exactly max_len window returns the space position, not the whole string
data.tailscale is available in the onboarding component from startup fetch; can be used to auto-fill public base URL for Teams
Agent tools access Teams Graph API via MsTeamsPlugin::graph_client() helper that returns (reqwest::Client, Secret<String>); edit/delete go through new public plugin methods delegating to outbound

workflow: When adding pub(crate) API methods that are not yet called, use #[allow(dead_code)] to avoid clippy warnings rather than forcing artificial callers
tokio::spawn closures must be Send + 'static — clone Arcs before the spawn block, never capture RwLock guards
Biome is used for JS linting (biome check), not eslint
Cargo workspace deps must be added to root Cargo.toml [workspace.dependencies] before individual crate Cargo.toml can reference them
Full compilation check workflow: cargo check --workspace, then cargo test, then cargo clippy, then cargo fmt --check
Commit before merging main to preserve branch work; 'git merge main' was clean with no conflicts after committing
PR creation via gh pr create with HEREDOC body formatting

friction: Multiple compilation cycles needed: SendOutcome visibility, RwLockWriteGuard Send issue, tokio::spawn clone pattern — each required a separate read-fix-compile loop
outbound.rs had mime_type field but code was written with content_type, requiring a grep-and-fix pass
Clippy produced many warnings (field_reassign_with_default, collapsible_if, unwrap_used in tests) requiring a subagent fix pass
chunking.rs test expectation was wrong after implementation, caught only at test run time
RedactedConfig hardcoded field count bug was only caught during a follow-up audit, not during initial implementation
graph.rs had a clamp-style clippy lint that was only caught in the final clippy pass
Teams Developer Portal link was missing from CLI bootstrap print_setup_links — caught in follow-up audit pass
The onboarding public URL requirement problem was only raised by the user after PR creation, not caught during implementation review
open_items: Localization (fr/zh) for Teams channel strings — intentionally deferred as a cross-cutting concern affecting all channels
E2E Playwright tests for Teams channel setup — no channel currently has dedicated e2e setup tests
Wiring poll vote tracking with persistent conversation store (in-memory only currently)
Per-team/channel config overrides UI in the edit modal (backend supports it, UI does not expose nested team/channel config)
Graph API permissions documentation — teams.md lists required permissions but there is no automated check or warning when Graph features are used without the right scopes configured
Feedback (thumbs up/down) Adaptive Cards with reflection prompt and cooldown — listed in comparison but not implemented
Welcome card for group chats using group_welcome_card config field — config exists but group welcome logic may need verification
